info.name: Nova.CommonUX.Api
info.description: CommonUX API endpoints covering authentication, configuration, and user profile management.

exec: // Generates a fresh HS256 token if none exists or the existing one is
// within 60 seconds of expiry. Reads from environment / collection vars.
// Required variables: jwt_secret, tenant_id, company_code, branch_code, user_id
// Skip token generation for anonymous endpoints.
// are NOT anonymous — they require a Bearer token and are not skipped.
// Reuse the existing token if it is still valid (60 s buffer).
console.log('[Nova.CommonUX] access_token still valid — skipping refresh.');
} catch (_) { /* malformed token — fall through to regenerate */ }
throw new Error('[Nova.CommonUX] Missing variable: ' + key +
'. Add it to your active environment or collection variables.');
// Write to collection variables (takes precedence over globals).
// Also write to environment if one is active.

request.description:
Machine-to-machine token endpoint. Issues a JWT for the application instance using a tenant client secret verified via Argon2id.

client_secret is the plaintext value you chose when seeding nova_auth.tenant_secrets:
  1. Generate hash:  nova-cipher argon2 <your-secret>
  2. Insert hash into tenant_secrets.client_secret_hash
  3. Set {{client_secret}} in your environment to that same plaintext value

See: src/services/Nova.CommonUX.Api/docs/nova-commonux-api-guide.md → Seeding Required Data → 1. tenant_secrets

Does NOT require a Bearer token — this is the bootstrap endpoint.
✅ Auto-auth: The post-response script automatically sets {{access_token}} on success.

response.name: 429 Too Many Requests

request.description:
Log in a user. Does NOT receive auto-injected context fields. When requires_2fa is true, use the session_token with Verify 2FA.

✅ Auto-auth: The post-response script automatically sets {{access_token}} on success (when token is non-empty).

response.name: 422 Unprocessable Entity
429 Too Many Requests

request.description:
Verify a TOTP 2FA code using the session_token from the login response.

✅ Auto-auth: The post-response script automatically sets {{access_token}} on success.

response.name: 422 Unprocessable Entity
429 Too Many Requests

request.description: Request a password reset email for the given User ID.

response.name: Success
429 Too Many Requests

request.description: Reset password using the token from the reset email.

response.name: 422 Unprocessable Entity
429 Too Many Requests

request.description: Send a magic link to the user's email for passwordless login.

response.name: Success
422 Unprocessable Entity
429 Too Many Requests

request.description:
Verify a magic link token to complete passwordless login.

✅ Auto-auth: The post-response script automatically sets {{access_token}} on success.

response.name: 429 Too Many Requests

request.description: Initiate social auth flow. Provider can be 'google', 'microsoft', or 'apple'. Redirect the user to the returned URL.

response.name: 422 Unprocessable Entity
429 Too Many Requests

request.description:
Complete the social auth flow after the OAuth callback returns a social_token. Requires tenant_id, provider, and social_token.

✅ Auto-auth: The post-response script automatically sets {{access_token}} on success.

response.name: 429 Too Many Requests

request.description:
Refresh an expiring JWT token. Requires a valid Bearer token in the Authorization header and the opaque refresh_token from the login/magic-link/social response. Returns a new JWT and a rotated refresh_token.

✅ Auto-auth: The post-response script automatically sets {{access_token}} on success.

response.name: 422 Unprocessable Entity
429 Too Many Requests

request.description: Authenticated user initiates linking a new social provider. Requires Bearer token. Returns an OAuth redirect URL. State param encodes the callback URL + authenticated identity so the complete step can resolve the account.

response.name: 422 Unprocessable Entity

request.description: Authenticated user completes linking a social provider. Requires Bearer token. tenant_id and user_id are read from JWT claims — do not send them in the body. Returns 409 if this provider is already linked to any account.

response.name: 409 Conflict

request.description: Fetch tenant configuration. Context fields are auto-injected by the frontend but must be included manually in Postman.

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "tenant_id": "T001",
